content: Imagine you're building a LEGO castle, and you need instructions to make sure it looks just right. Inside your body, there are tiny things called cells that need instructions too! These instructions come from a special set of plans called DNA. But how do the cells get these plans? That's where mRNA comes in. mRNA is like a messenger that carries the instructions from the DNA to the rest of the cell. It's like a special delivery truck that brings the LEGO instructions to the builders. The cell then uses these instructions to make the right proteins, which are like the LEGO bricks that build your body. Without mRNA, the cells wouldn't know what to do, and your body wouldn't be able to grow and work properly. So, mRNA is like a tiny messenger that helps your body build itself, one instruction at a time!
